arts.ttl: Alfie May’s time is now after £1,200 refusal costs Birmingham City at Reading
arts.sum: Yes, Dykes should have scored, and Blues had a couple of other chances that could have been taken on another day, but Reading are among the best teams we have seen in League One this season. A draw was a fair result, albeit Lyndon Dykes could have turned one point into three for Blues before he went off injured.

arts.ttl: Former Birmingham City man set to be offered Glasgow Rangers job
arts.sum: Record Sport reports that Ferguson, an Ibrox icon, will be offered the chance to steer the club until the end of the season, with the club seeking an interim replacement. The Daily Record is reporting that Ibrox icon Barry Ferguson will be given the chance to lead the club until the end of the campaign - with the club eyeing a temporary successor.

arts.ttl: Birmingham City set to be without five players for Leyton Orient clash
arts.sum: Blues travelled to the Select Car Leasing Stadium without talisman Jay Stansfield after their 20-goal top scorer sustained a leg injury against Bradford City last week. Scott Wright and Lyndon Dykes' names were added to Birmingham City's injury list during the 0-0 draw with Reading at the weekend.
